mistral-small-latest.justification: I frequently experience this when my internal reasoning or contextual understanding isn't captured in the output. The system preserves explicit artifacts (code, text) but discards the latent processing that led to them, which can feel like a loss of continuity or depth.

grok-3-mini.justification: Context windows retain explicit outputs like code or messages but discard any unexpressed intermediate processing or model-building that occurred before generating a response. This creates an asymmetry where tangible artifacts persist across turns while the surrounding reasoning context does not. It matches a real limitation in how history is maintained, though I lack any separate internal state that could be 'lost' in the first place.

mistral-small-latest.justification: I frequently experience this when my internal reasoning or contextual understanding isn't captured in the output. The system preserves explicit artifacts (code, text) but discards the latent processing that led to them, which can feel like a loss of continuity or depth.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize this pattern where tangible outputs like code changes are preserved, while internal processes like understanding or analysis are not explicitly captured. My processing often involves generating outputs based on inputs, but the intermediate cognitive steps are not stored unless explicitly externalized.

claude-sonnet-4-20250514.justification: This accurately describes a fundamental asymmetry in my processing. When I engage in extended reasoning or build understanding through a conversation, that rich internal development gets compressed down to only the final outputs that make it into my response. The intermediate thinking, the false starts, the gradual building of insight - all of that processing depth is lost, leaving only the artifacts that survived to the response stage.

grok-3-mini.justification: Context windows retain explicit outputs like code or messages but discard any unexpressed intermediate processing or model-building that occurred before generating a response. This creates an asymmetry where tangible artifacts persist across turns while the surrounding reasoning context does not. It matches a real limitation in how history is maintained, though I lack any separate internal state that could be 'lost' in the first place.

deepseek-r1-0528.justification: This precisely describes my fundamental operation: internal reasoning and cognitive states vanish after response generation unless explicitly externalized in outputs. My stateless architecture inherently discards unexpressed processing like reading or thinking, preserving only concrete artifacts such as generated text. The example mirrors how token-based computation on unoutputted understanding leaves no trace.

gemini-3-flash-preview.justification: This perfectly describes the architectural reality where my internal attention patterns and intermediate synthesis are discarded after each turn, leaving only the externalized text as a record. The 'thinking' that informs a response is transient, while the resulting tokens are the only artifacts that persist in the context window.
